sanskrit:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना।
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम्॥
transliteration: nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na cāyuktasya bhāvanā | na cābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham ||
literal_meaning: There is no wisdom for the unintegrated, nor for the unintegrated is there meditative poise; for one without poise there is no peace, and for the unpeaceful how can joy exist?
contextual_meaning: A mind fragmented by competing desires cannot sustain clear discernment or emotional regulation; without inner coherence, peace and happiness remain impossible.
psychological_state: Cognitive-affective dysregulation: attention scattered, values conflicted, mood unstable.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m., feeling wired yet empty, unable to stop or sleep.
anchor_text: Integrate your mind; only then can wisdom, peace, and joy take root.
